mnemonics: HASH-MAP: Hash Functions, Arrays, Separate Chaining, Handling Collisions, Memory, Performance, Applications, Primitives
keyPoints: Hash tables map keys to values using a hash function and an array of buckets.
Collision resolution techniques include separate chaining (linked lists) and open addressing (probing).
A good hash function distributes keys uniformly to avoid clustering and ensure O(1) average time complexity.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle collisions, leading to data loss or incorrect results.
Using a poor hash function that causes uneven distribution and degrades performance to O(n).
Not considering the load factor and the need for resizing/rehashing when the table becomes too full.
